metadata.title: Score a Build Plan Using the Boondoggle Score with Claude Code
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: Before Claude sees a single prompt, you know which step is highest-risk, which handoff is weakest, and which supervisory capacity you're most likely to skip.
A handoff condition must be machine-checkable — it names a test file, a count, or an artifact. Not: Claude finishes. Not: looks good. A specific assertion that exits zero or doesn't.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A handoff condition must be machine-checkable — it names a test file, a count, or an artifact. Not: Claude finishes. Not: looks good. A specific assertion that

beats.narration_text: We ask Claude to generate a Boondoggle Score table for Phase 1 of an application tracker.

props.command:
claude "Generate Boondoggle Score table for app tracker Phase 1:
  columns: step#, phase, labor, supervisory capacity, handoff condition
  flag: critical path and highest-risk handoff
  rule: every handoff condition must be machine-checkable"

beats.narration_text: Claude returns five steps. Every handoff condition names a pytest file or artifact count. The critical path connects the data model to the UI to the state persistence. The highest-risk handoff is step 3 — state persistence is the hardest to test without running the full app.

props.code:
# Boondoggle Score — App Tracker Phase 1

| # | Phase        | Labor  | Capacity | Handoff Condition                        |
|---|--------------|--------|----------|------------------------------------------|
| 1 | Data model   | Claude | —        | pytest tests/test_models.py exits 0      |
| 2 | Add form     | Claude | PA       | pytest tests/test_add.py -k happy        |
| 3 | State pers.  | Claude | PA+IJ    | all 3 apps survive reload in browser     | <- HIGH RISK
| 4 | Delete       | Human  | PF       | pytest tests/test_delete.py exits 0      |
| 5 | UI polish    | Human  | IJ       | screenshot matches wireframe.png         |

Critical path: step 1 -> 2 -> 3 (state persistence blocks all others)

beats.narration_text: The Boondoggle Score table — five steps, critical path highlighted, highest-risk row at step 3. State persistence is the step every other step inherits from.
We deliberately set row 1's handoff condition to 'Claude completes the data model' and re-run the scorer.

props.command: claude "Set row 1 handoff condition to: 'Claude completes the data model'.\nRe-run scorer. Does it flag this condition as invalid?\nExplain why it fails the machine-checkable rule."

beats.narration_text: The validator flags it: not machine-checkable. Then shows the corrected version with pytest.
The Boondoggle Score is a diagnostic before a single line of code is written. The highest-risk row is always the one that every other row inherits from.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The Boondoggle Score is a diagnostic before a single line of code is written. The highest-risk row is always the one that every other row inherits from.

beats.narration_text: Next: run the three-pass verification protocol.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Next: run the three-pass verification protocol.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At nikbearbrown.com.
